explanation: Conversation topics have drifted from the stated objective
conversation_text: Hey B, I was reading this article about kids these days using AI for everything, even homework. Don't you think it's messing up their brains? Back in our day, we had to memorize stuff. Yeah, A, totally. My nephew's in high school, uses ChatGPT for essays. Teachers can't even tell anymore. What happened to good old books? Exactly. My grandma always said, 'The ancestors didn't have computers, and they built pyramids. What's our excuse?' Tradition worked for thousands of years. Haha, pyramids, nice. But uh, isn't tech making life easier? Like, medicine and stuff? Sure, medicine's good, but... my dad told me, elders in the village said knowledge comes from the soil and stories passed down. Not from some machine spitting out answers. Remember how we learned farming from grandpa?
